#include <MAX31856MUD+/MAX31856MUD+Driver.hpp.bak>
#include "stm32g4xx_hal.h"

// Note: Ensure your hardware specific hspi is externally defined or passed properly.
// This typically comes from your auto-generated main.c (CubeMX).
extern SPI_HandleTypeDef hspi1; 

// Driver object instance
MAX31856Driver thermo;

#if 0
int main(void) {
    // HAL initialization (Usually done by generated main.c code)
    HAL_Init();
    
    // ... SystemClock_Config(), MX_GPIO_Init(), MX_SPI1_Init() would go here ...
    // Make sure the GPIO Clock for your CS pin port is enabled beforehand!

    // Initialize the driver with the SPI handle, CS Port, and CS Pin
    // Example uses Port A, Pin 4 as Chip Select
    thermo.Init(&hspi1, GPIOA, GPIO_PIN_4);

    // Optional Configuration: Configure CR0 and CR1 
    // e.g. thermo.SetCR0(0x80); // Set to automatic conversion mode
    // e.g. thermo.SetCR1(0x03); // Set to K-type thermocouple (default is K-type)

    float current_temperature = 0.0f;
    uint8_t faults = 0;

    // Main loop
    while (1) {
        // Fetch the temperature from the thermocouple registers
        current_temperature = thermo.ReadThermocoupleTempC();
        
        // Check for any fault conditions (open circuit, over/under voltage, etc.)
        faults = thermo.GetFaultStatus();

        // Delay 1 second between reads to keep it simple
        HAL_Delay(1000);
    }
}
#endif
